title: Light Japanese Skirt Steak Stir-fry
description: A fresh and light Japanese dish featuring skirt steak, served as a sick-day meal due to its low-fat content.

ingredients.name: soy sauce
ingredients.notes: low-sodium or tamari for vegan option

instructions: Preheat a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat. Heat the sesame oil and add the thinly sliced skirt steak. Cook until browned, about 2 minutes per side.
Add minced ginger and garlic to the pan; cook for an additional minute until fragrant but not burnt.
Push the steak aside and stir in red bell pepper strips. Cook for another 2-3 minutes or until slightly softened.
Return the steak to the wok with vegetables, pour in soy sauce (or tamari), cover and simmer gently for about 1 minute.
Sprinkle chopped green onions on top and serve hot.
